amendments.normalized_body: In `json_fill_none` (fs.rs:2284-2300), the `entry().or_insert(value)` merge silently prefers base over overlay for ALL fields, including any future optional fields that may need conflict detection. Currently, conflict checks for run_id, plan_hash, and outcome_detail are performed explicitly before calling `json_fill_none`, but if a future optional field with conflict semantics is added to StartJournalDetails or CompletionJournalDetails, the merge will silently keep the stale base value without flagging the conflict. Add a code comment at the `json_fill_none` call sites (fs.rs:2326 and fs.rs:2367) documenting the invariant: any new optional field with conflict semantics MUST have an explicit conflict check added to `merge()` above the `json_fill_none` call, otherwise conflicting values will be silently preserved from the existing record rather than flagged as an error.

`src/adapters/fs.rs:2957-2961` only auto-populates `plan_hash` when a brand-new start row is created; the completion path for an existing row still skips snapshot backfill entirely (`src/adapters/fs.rs:3231-3264`, and terminal replays use the same caller-only backfill helper at `src/adapters/fs.rs:2672-2692`). `record_bead_completion`/`update_task_run` also still forward `plan_hash` straight through from the caller (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:557-563`). The result is that any in-flight row that was created without `plan_hash` still finalizes with `plan_hash: None` even when the milestone snapshot already has the canonical hash. I reproduced this by pre-seeding a running `TaskRunEntry` with `plan_hash=None`, setting `snapshot.plan_hash="plan-v1"`, and then calling `record_bead_completion(..., plan_hash=None)`: the stored row remained `None`. That still loses lineage for legacy/incomplete starts, so issue 3 is only partially fixed.

final_accepted_amendments.normalized_body: In `json_fill_none` (fs.rs:2284-2300), the `entry().or_insert(value)` merge silently prefers base over overlay for ALL fields, including any future optional fields that may need conflict detection. Currently, conflict checks for run_id, plan_hash, and outcome_detail are performed explicitly before calling `json_fill_none`, but if a future optional field with conflict semantics is added to StartJournalDetails or CompletionJournalDetails, the merge will silently keep the stale base value without flagging the conflict. Add a code comment at the `json_fill_none` call sites (fs.rs:2326 and fs.rs:2367) documenting the invariant: any new optional field with conflict semantics MUST have an explicit conflict check added to `merge()` above the `json_fill_none` call, otherwise conflicting values will be silently preserved from the existing record rather than flagged as an error.

payload.summary: Final review accepted 1 amendment(s); restart required.
